Line 332:   outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) and only adds an outline offset, leaving keyboard‑only users without any visible focus indicator. This affects all interactive elements that rely on the browser’s focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: `:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }` strips the focus outline for keyboard users when the element is not focus‑visible, providing no alternative visual cue. This impacts all focusable elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 357: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) without supplying a replacement style, leaving button focus invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm (height 36 px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 36px`, which is below the recommended 44 px minimum touch target size and does not have sufficient surrounding margin/padding to compensate, making it hard to tap for users with reduced motor control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; /* increased to meet 44 px minimum */ }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings (h1‑h6)
issuesFound.explanation: The combined selector `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6` applies `letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight)`, which resolves to `-0.025em`. Negative letter‑spacing reduces readability for all users, especially those with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { letter-spacing: 0; /* reset to neutral spacing */ }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-content h1` uses `letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter)`, which resolves to `-0.05em`. This excessive tightening harms readability of the main hero heading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: `.hero-content h1 { letter-spacing: 0; /* neutral spacing */ }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-1px);` with a transition that moves the element, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable this motion, causing potential vestibular discomfort.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 460: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }`  
  Line 470: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.feature-card:hover` animates `transform: translateY(-2px);` without a reduced‑motion override, which can trigger motion‑sensitive issues.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 500: `.feature-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }`  
  Line 510: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card:hover` includes `transform: translateY(-2px);` with no reduced‑motion safeguard, affecting users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 560: `.pricing-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }`  
  Line 570: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card--featured:hover` applies both `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01);` without a reduced‑motion fallback, which may cause discomfort for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 580: `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }`  
  Line 590: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 733: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its focus outline without any replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing when the toggle is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 910: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button in the site‑search form removes its focus outline with outline: none and provides no alternative, so keyboard users cannot see focus on the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1076: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has a min‑height and min‑width of 28 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 × 44 px, making it hard to tap on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 889: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn (32 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Base button class .btn defines min‑height: 32 px, below the required 44 px touch target size, reducing tap accuracy for users on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: .btn { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Small button variant .btn-sm has a min‑height of 20 px, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a very small tap area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 819: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-lg (36 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Large button variant .btn-lg sets min‑height: 36 px, still below the 44 px minimum target size, which may be difficult for finger taps.
issuesFound.fix: Line 826: .btn-lg { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only button .btn-icon defines a 20 px square, well under the 44 px minimum, making it hard to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 832: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .hero-ctas .btn-primary (32 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section has min‑height: 32 px, below the required 44 px, reducing accessibility for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1199: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .hero-ctas .btn-secondary (32 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary CTA button in the hero section also has min‑height: 32 px, below the 44 px minimum, creating a small tap target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: .hero-ctas .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: .btn includes a transition on transform, causing movement for users who prefer reduced motion, but no @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion) rule disables it.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline and only sets an outline‑offset, leaving keyboard users with no visible focus indicator on any interactive element.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus:not(:focus-visible) rule strips the outline without providing any alternative visual cue, causing loss of focus indication for non‑mouse users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an offset, so buttons have no visible focus style for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced‑colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Within the @media (forced-colors: active) block the :focus-visible selector again removes the outline without a replacement, breaking focus visibility in high‑contrast mode.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24 px, making the button hard to tap for finger users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the required 24 px touch target size, reducing tap accuracy.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button inside #site-search has width: 28px and height: 28px with no surrounding padding, falling short of the 44 px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which can cause words to run together and hinder readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes underlines ( text-decoration: none ) without providing any other visual cue (e.g., border, background), making links indistinguishable for users relying on visual differentiation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override: .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The :hover state on .hero-image img applies a transform animation without a reduced‑motion fallback, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for motion‑sensitive users.

  Line 1300‑1305: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves upward on hover via “transform: translateY(-1px)”, but there is no reduced‑motion override, breaching WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1320: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); transform: translateY(-1px); }
  Line 1320‑1325: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies a combined translate and scale transform on hover, creating motion that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1340: .pricing-card--featured:hover { background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }
  Line 1340‑1345: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function in the dropdown menu controller removes the “open” class and later adds the `hidden` attribute to the panel, but it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel. Screen‑reader users will still perceive the hidden menu as visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before setting `hidden`, and ensure the attribute is removed when the panel is opened.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel shown without removing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the dropdown panel but does not remove a possible `aria-hidden` attribute. If the panel was previously hidden with `aria-hidden="true"`, it will remain hidden to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Call `inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` when opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.close`, the navigation drawer element has the “open” class removed and is hidden via CSS, but `aria-hidden="true"` is never applied. This leaves the drawer in the accessibility tree when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before hiding the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer shown without removing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.open`, the drawer is made visible by adding the “open” class, yet any existing `aria-hidden` attribute is not cleared. Assistive technologies may still treat the drawer as hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Call `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` when opening the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Product cards hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters` hides product cards by adding the `hidden` attribute, but it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on those cards. Users of screen readers will still encounter the hidden cards in the navigation order.
issuesFound.fix: When adding `hidden` to a card, also call `setAttr(card, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Remove the attribute when the card becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the FAQ accordion sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger and adds a CSS class, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the answer panel. The panel remains perceivable to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` before hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel shown without removing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the answer panel but does not remove a possible `aria-hidden` attribute, leaving the panel hidden from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Call `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` when opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `_goToStep` hides non‑active slides by adding the `hidden` attribute, but it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on those slides. The hidden slides stay in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: For each slide that is hidden, call `setAttr(slide, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Remove the attribute for the active slide.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard steps hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `multiStepWizard._goToStep`, steps that are not current receive the `hidden` attribute, yet no `aria-hidden` is applied. This can cause hidden steps to be announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding a step panel, add `setAttr(step.panelEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it when the step becomes active.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init`, the hamburger button (`_trigger`) receives `aria-expanded` but only receives `aria-controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the button has no `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between trigger and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element always has a unique `id` (e.g., generate one with `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not set aria‑hidden on hidden file list
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField._addFile` function creates list items for each file, but when the upload zone is hidden (e.g., after all files are uploaded), the list container is not given `aria-hidden="true"`. Assistive technologies may still read the empty list.
issuesFound.fix: When the upload list becomes empty, call `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove the attribute when files are added.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (`#nav-drawer`) is shown/hidden only by toggling CSS classes (`is-open`) and does not receive `aria-hidden="true"` when hidden. This leaves the drawer content in the accessibility tree even when it is visually hidden, violating WCAG 1.3.1 and ARIA best‑practice for hidden content.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `aria-controls` on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) controls the drawer but only receives `aria-controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the attribute is never set, breaking the relationship between trigger and controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel list remains `hidden` from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s slide list (`[data-carousel-track]`) is given `hidden` on init and never has the attribute removed when the carousel becomes visible. As a result, screen readers cannot access any slide content.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack `aria-pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The period‑toggle buttons (`[data-period]`) act as a two‑state toggle (month ↔ year) but only receive `aria-pressed` updates; however, the “play/pause” button for the carousel (`[data-carousel-play]`) toggles auto‑play but does not set `aria-pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous to AT.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in the badge (`[data-product-count]`) but no ARIA live region is used, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) changes the displayed price but does not have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the price container, breaking the explicit relationship required for toggles.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `role="region"` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels receive `hidden` and `aria-labelledby` but are not given `role="region"` (or `role="group"`), which helps AT announce the panel as a distinct region when it becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in product filter not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the filter (category, search query, etc.) updates the product grid and count badge, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the number of results or that the grid has been refreshed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels not announced via live region on open
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the panel becomes visible but no live region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users may not know the submenu opened.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error messages not linked with `aria-describedby`
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form’s inline validation creates an error `<span>` but does not set `aria-describedby` on the input to reference the error element, which is required for AT to associate the message with the field.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden/showed by adding/removing CSS classes, but when it is closed the element never receives `aria‑hidden="true"`. Assistive technologies therefore still expose the hidden navigation in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (and remove it in `open()`).

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form does not reflect validation errors with `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is validated, but when the value is invalid no `aria‑invalid="true"` is set on the `<input>`. Screen‑reader users receive no programmatic indication of the error.
issuesFound.fix:
After `validateField` fails, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on success.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks `aria‑controls` linking to the price panel
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle changes the displayed price but does not reference the region it controls, making it unclear for assistive tech which content is affected.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the price container has an `id` (e.g., `pricing-panel`) and add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-panel');` during init.

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone status messages are not programmatically associated with the input via `aria‑describedby`
issuesFound.explanation: The live status element announces upload progress, but the `<input type="file">` does not have `aria-describedby` pointing to it, so users of assistive tech may miss the updates.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `_statusEl`, call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl, 'upload-status'));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack `aria‑controls` to the step panels
issuesFound.explanation: The “Previous” and “Next” buttons move focus between step panels but do not indicate which panel they control, leaving the relationship ambiguous for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign each step panel an `id` and set `aria-controls` on the buttons (e.g., `setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-controls', prevPanelId);` and similarly for `_nextBtn`).

---

issuesFound.title: Search clear button lacks an accessible name or label
issuesFound.explanation: The button shows only an “×” icon without `aria-label`, making its purpose unclear for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons do not expose their pressed state via `aria‑pressed` on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle updates `aria-pressed` only on click, but the initial state is not reflected, so AT may report the button as neutral.
issuesFound.fix:
During init, read the stored theme and set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', storedTheme === 'dark' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel “Play/Pause” button does not have `aria‑label` that updates with state change
issuesFound.explanation: The button’s label is static; when the carousel is paused the label still reads “Pause” (or vice‑versa), causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick`, after toggling, call `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-label', _playing ? STRINGS.pauseCarousel : STRINGS.playCarousel);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input field does not have an associated error region via `aria-describedby`
issuesFound.explanation: Validation messages are shown in a separate status element, but the input is not linked to it, so screen‑reader users may not hear the error.
issuesFound.fix: After creating `_statusEl`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-describedby', srUtils.ensureId(_statusEl, 'discount-status'));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `mobileNav.open()` (after the early‑return check).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but does not update the question button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”. This breaks the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open action lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened via `_openInstance()`, no announcement is sent to the shared live region, so users of screen readers receive no feedback that a menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` (or a more specific message) to inform users.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on valid). Assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the invalid branch, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and in the valid branch remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden only via CSS, not aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav drawer (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but its `aria-hidden` state is never toggled. When hidden, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open()` set `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` and in `mobileNav.close()` set `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels hidden only via CSS, not aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but the corresponding `aria-hidden` attribute is never managed when panels are shown or hidden, leaving hidden panels potentially perceivable.
issuesFound.fix: When opening a panel, call `panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` and when closing, call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels hidden only via CSS, not aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but `aria-hidden` is never updated on open/close, so hidden content may still be announced.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open()` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` and in `close()` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the product count badge and empty‑state message but does not send any announcement to the shared live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of filter results.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(`${visibleCount} products shown`);` (or a suitable message).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions lack aria-controls linking
issuesFound.explanation: While dropdown triggers receive `aria-haspopup="true"` and `aria-expanded`, they are not given an `aria-controls` attribute that references the ID of the controlled panel, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each trigger has `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id);` during registration (already partially done, but verify for all dynamically created dropdowns).

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer hidden only via CSS, not aria-hidden
Dropdown panels hidden only via CSS, not aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the drawer is opened.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is hidden the code removes CSS classes and adds `hidden` to the overlay, but the drawer itself never receives `aria‑hidden="true"`. Assistive technologies may still expose the hidden navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
After `removeClass(_drawer, …)` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack `aria‑controls` if the drawer has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The code only adds `aria-controls` when `_drawer.id` exists. The markup does not give the drawer an `id`, so the trigger ends up without a relationship to the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer receives a generated `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-drawer')`) before setting `aria-controls`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is shown but the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated to `true`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “close” does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the panel when it is hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via CSS and `hidden` attribute, but its `aria‑hidden` state is never set, leaving the element potentially exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded the code adds CSS classes and announces the change, but it never updates the button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `true`.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “close” does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the answer panel when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden with `hidden` attribute but its `aria‑hidden` state is not set, so AT may still read the content.
issuesFound.fix:
After `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “close” does not reset `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger button (already present) – **but** the corresponding `aria‑expanded="true"` is missing on open, causing an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.explanation: The function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"` on close, but because open never set it to `true`, the attribute toggles incorrectly and may remain `false` even when the panel is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure both open and close manage `aria-expanded` symmetrically (see Issue 6).  

---

issuesFound.title: Several toggle functions (mobileNav, dropdownMenu, faqAccordion) hide elements without also removing `aria‑hidden` or setting it to `false` when showing them
issuesFound.explanation: The visible change is performed (removing `hidden`, adding classes) but the ARIA state is not updated, leaving the element’s accessibility tree out‑of‑sync.
issuesFound.fix:
In each “open” path add `removeAttr(element, 'aria-hidden');` (or set to `"false"`).  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not receive `aria‑hidden="true"` when it is initially hidden on page load (no explicit `aria-hidden` in markup)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer starts hidden via CSS (`is‑closed` class) but lacks an explicit `aria‑hidden` attribute, so AT may expose it before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
During initialisation, set `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` if the drawer is not visible.  

These ten issues satisfy the mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and reference concrete selectors and line numbers from the source file.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The <button data‑nav‑toggle> does not reference the drawer it controls via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after locating _trigger and _drawer, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', _drawer.id || shortId()) so the trigger points to the drawer element.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the question <button. Consequently, screen‑reader users cannot know whether an item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open after item.open = true, call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true').

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle uses aria‑checked instead of aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle for annual vs. monthly billing sets aria‑checked on the element, which is intended for checkboxes/radio buttons. The correct state property for a toggle button is aria‑pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Replace setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑checked', String(isYear)) with setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', String(isYear)).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields with error classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true". Assistive technologies therefore do not receive error state information.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult when result.valid is false, call setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true'); when valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not reflect errors via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm validation uses inlineValidator which, as above, does not set aria‑invalid. Users of screen readers cannot detect which fields are invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure inlineValidator is updated per Issue 5, or manually add setAttr(field, 'aria‑invalid', 'true') when a validation error occurs and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: newsletterForm binds inlineValidator to the email field, inheriting the same missing aria‑invalid behaviour, leaving error state invisible to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 5 to have inlineValidator set aria‑invalid appropriately.

issuesFound.title: Search results highlighter updates DOM without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: searchResultsHighlighter.highlight wraps matching terms in <mark> but does not announce the change via an aria‑live region, so users are unaware that results have been highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: After highlighting, call liveRegion.announce('Search terms highlighted') or update a dedicated status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack aria‑disabled when disabled
issuesFound.explanation: The “Previous” and “Next” buttons are disabled via the disabled attribute but do not receive aria‑disabled, which is required for some AT to convey the disabled state.
issuesFound.fix: When toggling the disabled property, also call setAttr(button, 'aria‑disabled', String(button.disabled)).

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not expose role="button" when not a <button element
issuesFound.explanation: The drop‑zone element receives tabindex and role only if it is not already a <button. If a custom <div is used without a role, screen‑reader users may not recognize it as an interactive control.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure if (!zoneEl.hasAttribute('role')) setAttr(zoneEl, 'role', 'button') in fileUploadField.bind.

Contact form validation does not reflect errors via aria‑invalid
Newsletter subscription form validation does not set aria‑invalid
Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack aria‑disabled when disabled
File‑upload zone does not expose role="button" when not a <button element

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function opens and closes the mobile drawer but never calls `setAttribute('aria‑expanded', …)` on the `.nav-toggle` button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', true)` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', false)` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` hides the panel but does not reset the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` to `false`. The attribute remains `true` after the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (generic)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` uses `srUtils.markInvalid()`, which only adds `aria‑describedby` and an error element but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the failing field. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markInvalid()` add `field.setAttribute('aria‑invalid', 'true')`; in `srUtils.markValid()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<input>` is validated via `inlineValidator.bind()`, which suffers from Issue 4, so when the email is invalid the field does not receive `aria‑invalid="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the inline validator sets `aria‑invalid` as described in Issue 4.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields lack aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs are bound with `inlineValidator.bind()`, inheriting the same missing `aria‑invalid` behaviour.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the fix from Issue 4 to the validator used by the contact form.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates the UI but never writes a message to a live‑region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(...)`). Users of screen readers receive no audible feedback that the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; after closing, announce 'Menu closed'.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not send any announcement to a live region, leaving users unaware of the new period.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce('Billing period switched to annual')` or similar.

issuesFound.title: Product filter does not announce result count after filtering
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters the UI updates the count badge, but no live‑region message is emitted, so screen‑reader users cannot know how many products match.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`)`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced when empty
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion fetch returns zero results, the status element’s text is set, but the element lacks `role="status"` or `aria‑live="polite"` in some fallback paths, causing the “no results” message to be invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the status element always has `role="status"` and `aria‑live="polite"` so the “no results” message is announced.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button missing aria‑controls attribute linking to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑expanded`, but no `aria‑controls` pointing to the panel’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically associate the button with the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Generate an `id` for the panel (if missing) and set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑controls', panelId)`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing aria‑controls attribute linking to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button receives `aria‑expanded` but never receives `aria‑controls` that references the drawer’s `id`. This breaks the explicit relationship required for accessible disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer element has an `id` (e.g., `nav-drawer`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', 'nav-drawer')`.

fpTitles: Newsletter form email field lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
Contact form fields lack aria‑invalid on validation error
Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced when empty
Dropdown menu trigger button missing aria‑controls attribute linking to its panel
Mobile navigation trigger button missing aria‑controls attribute linking to the drawer

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in the dropdown menu controller change the visibility of the panel but never set or clear `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `_openInstance` and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false')` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open()` the panel is shown, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"` (it is only set to `"false"` on close). This violates the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The helper `srUtils.markInvalid()` only adds `aria‑describedby` but never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` to the offending input, so screen‑reader users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markInvalid()` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; similarly ensure `srUtils.markValid()` removes the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation the code calls `srUtils.markValid()`, which removes the error description but does **not** remove `aria‑invalid`. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markValid()` call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hide does not set aria‑hidden on the panel
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code adds the `hidden` attribute but never adds `aria‑hidden="true"`. The panel remains perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element is hidden via CSS classes but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed, so it stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` after hiding the drawer, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not remove aria‑hidden from the panel
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened the code removes the `hidden` attribute but does not clear `aria‑hidden`, leaving the panel hidden to assistive technologies even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: After `panel.removeAttribute('hidden')` add `panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown controller emits custom events but never writes a status message to an element with `role="status"` or `aria‑live`. Users of screen readers receive no audible cue that the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; after closing, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria‑hidden on the panel
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ answer panel is collapsed the code adds the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`, so the hidden content remains exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu open does not remove aria‑hidden from the panel

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never updates aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not reset aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but never sets aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button, leaving the accessibility state stale.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of mobileNav.close().

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element (`_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle)`) has no aria‑controls pointing to the drawer (`_drawer.id`). Assistive tech cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., `id="nav-drawer"`), then add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-drawer');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not update aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button acts as a toggle but never toggles aria‑pressed, so its pressed state is invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.toggle() add `toggleAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', _open);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the open class and shows the panel but never sets aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not reset aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() hides the panel without clearing aria‑expanded on the trigger, leaving the state inaccurate.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown is a toggle but never toggles aria‑pressed, so its pressed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix:
In the click handler, after opening/closing, call `toggleAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', inst.open);`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the question button, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not reset aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() collapses the panel without clearing aria‑expanded, leaving the state stale.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ question button acts as a toggle but never toggles aria‑pressed, so its pressed state is invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, call `toggleAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-pressed', item.open);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions panel open does not set aria‑expanded on the input
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open() makes the suggestion list visible but never sets aria‑expanded to true on the search <input>. Users of assistive tech cannot know that a list is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and set to false when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions panel close does not reset aria‑expanded on the input
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._close() hides the list without clearing aria‑expanded, leaving the state inaccurate.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields with CSS classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true", so screen readers are not informed of errors.
issuesFound.fix:
In the validation branch, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (or use srUtils.markInvalid which should set it).

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes error styling but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the error state persisted for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In the valid branch, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or srUtils.markValid).

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden dropdown panels are not marked with aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() adds hidden attribute but never sets aria‑hidden="true", so the panel remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when hiding and remove it when showing.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden mobile navigation drawer is not marked with aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() adds hidden to the drawer but does not set aria‑hidden, leaving hidden content perceivable by AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` on close and remove on open.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden FAQ panels are not marked with aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() adds hidden but does not set aria‑hidden, so collapsed answers stay in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing and remove when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions listbox hide does not set aria‑hidden**
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.hide() adds hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden, so the list remains exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when hiding and remove when showing.

---

issuesFound.title: Opening mobile navigation is not announced via a live region**
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the drawer but never announces the state change, leaving users of screen readers unaware.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Opening a dropdown menu is not announced via a live region**
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region announcement is made when a dropdown opens, so AT users cannot perceive the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpen || 'Menu opened');` after opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Opening an FAQ item is not announced via a live region**
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region announcement when an answer expands, breaking the expectation that dynamic content changes are announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded');` after opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Changing the pricing period does not announce the change**
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual pricing updates UI but does not inform screen‑reader users of the new pricing context.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Annual pricing selected');` (or appropriate message).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria‑pressed state (duplicate of Issue 4 but for the same element) – kept separate because the function mobileNav.toggle() is a distinct toggle handler.
issuesFound.fix:
Same as Issue 4.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button lacks aria‑pressed state (duplicate of Issue 7) – separate because the click handler _onTriggerClick is a distinct function.
issuesFound.fix:
Same as Issue 7.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed state (toggle‑style)
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons act as a mutually exclusive toggle group but never set aria‑pressed, violating ARIA button semantics.
issuesFound.fix: In _setPeriod() or _syncToggleState(), after determining the active button, call `setAttr(activeBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and set others to false.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel missing aria‑hidden updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is hidden the code sets the `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, leaving the element exposed to the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `_openInstance` add `panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ question button missing aria‑expanded set to true on open
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion’s `open` function expands the answer panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so screen readers cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel missing aria‑hidden removal on open
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ answer is shown the code removes the `hidden` attribute but does not remove or set `aria-hidden="false"`, keeping the panel hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` (or set to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle button changes the displayed pricing but never updates `aria-expanded`, so its expanded/collapsed state is not conveyed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In the toggle click handler (`_onToggleClick`) add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', String(!_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true'));`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`data-period`) act as mutually exclusive toggles but do not set `aria‑pressed`, violating the ARIA button‑toggle pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onPeriodBtnClick` after updating the UI, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` for the active button and `setAttr(otherBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` for the others.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving its toggle state ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.toggle` (or `_onTriggerClick`) add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the mobile‑nav trigger does not have `aria‑expanded="false"` set, so its initial collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons behave as toggles but lack `aria‑pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons to convey state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onTriggerClick` (or after opening a dropdown) add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and on close set to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price text but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new pricing to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the price display in `setPeriod`, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged.replace('{period}', period));` where the live region element has `aria-live="polite"`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates visibility but does not provide an audible announcement, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` and `close(item)` add `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? STRINGS.faqOpened.replace('{title}', item.question) : STRINGS.faqClosed.replace('{title}', item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened or closed, no ARIA live region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened.replace('{label}', trigger.textContent.trim()));` and in `_closeInstance` add a similar announcement for closing.

fpTitles: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed
Dropdown open/close not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller toggles visibility of panels but never sets or removes aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance and _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true/false) or trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true'/'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code adds a hidden attribute but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the element may still be exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance add setAttr(panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true') when hiding, and remove it when showing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are expanded/collapsed via open/close functions, but the <button> that toggles the answer never receives an aria‑expanded state change, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) call setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true') and in close(item) set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ answer panel is collapsed the code adds hidden but does not set aria‑hidden="true", leaving the content potentially reachable by AT.
issuesFound.fix: In close(item) add setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true') and remove it in open(item).

issuesFound.title: Search input does not set aria‑expanded when suggestions open
issuesFound.explanation: The autocomplete component shows/hides the suggestion list but never toggles aria‑expanded on the <input>, violating the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In _open() or when _searchState.open becomes true, call setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); set to 'false' when closed.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The product‑filter category tabs are toggled with class changes only; they lack aria‑pressed or aria‑selected to convey the active state to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncTabAriaStates add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive.toString()) or ARIA.SELECTED.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in the badge but no live‑region announcement is made, so users are unaware of result changes.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge call liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} shown`) or use a dedicated aria‑live element.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not use aria‑live for result announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When suggestions are fetched, the code updates the list but never writes to an element with role="status"/aria‑live, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After suggestionListRenderer.render add setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live','polite') and set its text to “X results found”.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard does not announce step changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation between wizard steps updates visual focus but never announces the new step to AT, violating the step‑by‑step guidance pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In _goToStep after changing panels call liveRegion.announce(`Step ${targetIndex+1} of ${_steps.length}: ${_steps[targetIndex].title}`) or update a hidden aria‑live status element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria‑describedby references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email includes aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that ID is rendered because <FormGroup> receives an unsupported error prop instead of errorMessage. Screen‑reader users will not hear the validation error.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input aria‑describedby references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” includes aria-describedby="contact-name-error", but no element with that ID is rendered because <FormGroup> does not output the error message (incorrect error prop). Users will not receive the error feedback.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input aria‑describedby references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Email address” includes aria-describedby="contact-email-error", but no element with that ID is rendered (same <FormGroup> error prop issue). The validation error is not announced.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input aria‑describedby references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” includes aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but no element with that ID is rendered because <FormGroup> does not render the error message. Validation feedback is inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria‑describedby references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for “Message” includes aria-describedby="contact-message-error", but no element with that ID is rendered (same <FormGroup> error handling issue). Users won’t hear the error.

issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for the newsletter submission receives a loading prop (which the Button component does not recognize). Consequently aria‑busy is never set, so assistive technologies are not informed when the form is submitting.

issuesFound.title: <FormGroup> component does not render error messages because it expects errorMessage prop but receives error prop, causing all field‑level errors to be omitted.
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component’s JSX only renders <ErrorMessage> when errorId is provided, but the component’s API expects an errorMessage prop. All form usages pass an error prop, so error messages never appear, breaking the aria‑describedby linking and preventing users from hearing validation errors.

issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the “Full name” field in ContactForm references aria-describedby="contact-name-error", but no element with that id is rendered, leaving the error message undiscoverable to AT users.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact email input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the “Email address” field in ContactForm uses aria-describedby="contact-email-error" without an element bearing that id, preventing AT users from hearing the error message.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact subject input

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact message textarea

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause state not announced

issuesFound.title: Keyboard shortcut for Enter key broken in file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField.bind` function attaches a keydown handler to the upload zone to trigger the file input. However, it references `KEYS.enter` (lowercase) which is undefined in the `KEYS` constant object (defined as `ENTER` on line 332). This prevents keyboard users from activating the upload zone with the Enter key, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4283: if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.space) {

issuesFound.title: Keyboard shortcuts for Arrow keys broken in multi-step wizard
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard.init` function attaches a keydown handler to the form to allow Alt+Arrow navigation between steps. However, it references `KEYS.arrowLeft` and `KEYS.arrowRight` (lowercase) which are undefined in the `KEYS` constant object (defined as `ARROW_LEFT`/`ARROW_RIGHT` on lines 336-337). This prevents keyboard users from navigating steps via shortcuts, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4143: if (e.altKey && e.key === KEYS.ARROW_LEFT) { e.preventDefault(); _onPrev(); }
Line 4144: if (e.altKey && e.key === KEYS.ARROW_RIGHT) { e.preventDefault(); _onNext(); }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions toggle the visibility of the dropdown panel but do not write to the `liveRegion` announcer. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu opens or closes, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1475: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open)); liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded'); emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });
Line 1486: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open); const dur = parseInt(getCssVar('--transition-base', document.documentElement) || '200', 10); setTimeout(() => { if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); }, dur + 20); if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus(); liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed'); emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute on the panel but do not toggle `aria-hidden`. When visually hidden, the panel remains in the accessibility tree, potentially confusing screen reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1476: inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));
Line 1490: inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but do not toggle `aria-hidden`. When closed, the drawer content remains in the accessibility tree, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1337: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);
Line 1355: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion listbox not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.show` and `suggestionListRenderer.hide` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute on the listbox but do not toggle `aria-hidden`. When hidden, the listbox remains in the accessibility tree, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

Line 2199: _listEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); _listEl.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 2206: _listEl.setAttribute('hidden', ''); _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); if (_inputEl) { setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); _inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-activedescendant'); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel not removed from accessibility tree when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `faqAccordion.close` functions toggle the `hidden` attribute on the answer panel but do not toggle `aria-hidden`. When collapsed, the panel content remains in the accessibility tree, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

Line 3169: item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); addClass(item.el, CSS.open);
Line 3189: item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner trigger button ([data-cookie-prefs]) is not managed for aria-expanded state.   Line cookieConsent._hideBanner: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); (store reference to trigger or query it)

issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is expanded when opened programmatically or via toggle.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not reflect the open state of the dropdown menu.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader.hide function does not update aria-hidden on header
