issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner appearance not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent _showBanner function displays the consent dialog but does not announce its appearance to screen readers. Users may not be aware the dialog has opened.

fpTitles: mobileNav overlay not updated in accessibility tree
dropdownMenu panel not updated in accessibility tree
suggestionListRenderer list not updated in accessibility tree
activeFilterChips removal not announced via live region
cookieConsent banner appearance not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and announces the state change, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function reveals the drawer by adding a CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="false" on the drawer element. If the drawer is hidden via CSS display:none by default, it remains in the accessibility tree as hidden when opened, or vice versa depending on CSS implementation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function sets aria-expanded="false", leading to incorrect state reporting for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function changes visible content by revealing the dropdown panel but does not write to an aria-live region to announce this change to screen reader users. Other modules like mobileNav and faqAccordion correctly announce their state changes.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel open and updates the URL hash, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", causing a mismatch in the reported state of the accordion item.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates function does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function toggles the CSS active class on filter tab buttons to indicate selection, but it does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen reader users rely on this attribute to identify which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. This prevents assistive technology from explicitly identifying the field as having an error state.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader _onScroll function does not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The stickyHeader._onScroll function toggles the header visibility using CSS classes (header--hidden) based on scroll direction. If these classes hide the element visually (e.g., display: none), the aria-hidden attribute should be updated to reflect this in the accessibility tree.

fpTitles: stickyHeader _onScroll function does not update aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) and update CSS classes, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1630: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line ~1655: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function initializes the mobile navigation but does not set the initial `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`). If the menu is closed by default, the attribute should be explicitly set to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1685: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions show/hide the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) using CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute on the overlay, but they do not toggle `aria-hidden` on the drawer element itself. When closed, the drawer should be removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1630: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line ~1655: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). This prevents assistive technologies from tracking the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1795: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line ~1810: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel and updates CSS classes, but it does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger (`item.trigger`). The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2675: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `faqAccordion.close` functions change visible content (expanding/collapsing answers) but do not announce this change via a live region. Only the search filter function (`_filterByQuery`) announces results. Users may miss that an answer has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2675: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));
  Line ~2705: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator marks fields invalid without aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function links an error message to the field via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1395: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid` function removes the error description but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute (or set it to "false"). The field may remain stuck in an invalid state in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1405: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function updates the product count badge (`_updateCountBadge`) dynamically when filters change, but no text is written to an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2360: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates price displays across all plan cards when the billing period toggles, but it does not announce this significant content change to an `aria-live` region. Users may not realize prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2565: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop.init` function attaches a click listener to `_btn` but does not verify if it is a native button. If it is a `div` or `span`, it lacks `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, and a keyboard handler for Enter/Space, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2075: if (_btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_btn, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(_btn, 'tabindex', '0'); _btn.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } }); }

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher.init` function delegates click events to `[data-theme-toggle]` elements without ensuring they are interactive for keyboard users. If these are not native buttons, they require `role`, `tabindex`, and keydown handlers to be accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3665: qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { if (btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(btn, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(btn, 'tabindex', '0'); } }); document.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.target.matches('[data-theme-toggle]') && (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ')) { e.preventDefault(); e.target.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner shown without announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._showBanner` function injects or reveals the consent dialog and traps focus, but it does not announce the appearance of the dialog to screen readers via a live region. Users may be confused by the sudden focus trap.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3545: liveRegion.announceAssertive('Cookie consent settings are now available.');

issuesFound.fix: Line 2648: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader does not manage aria-hidden when visually hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The _onScroll() function in stickyHeader adds a CSS class to hide the header visually but does not set aria-hidden="true". The header remains in the accessibility tree and may be focusable while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1804: setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, String(_hidden));

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal does not manage aria-hidden for revealed elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _reveal() function adds a visible class to elements but does not remove aria-hidden. If elements are initially hidden from the accessibility tree, they remain hidden after becoming visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3242: el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator updates text without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate() function updates the textContent of stat elements to show counting numbers but does not announce the final value or changes via a live region. Screen reader users may miss the updated statistics.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding the error state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not remove aria-invalid or set it to "false". Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is resolved.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and locks scroll but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes the open class but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the menu is closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and close functions toggle visibility classes but do not toggle aria-hidden on the _drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close instance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu register function does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is undefined for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the hidden attribute but do not toggle aria-hidden on the panel. The panel remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and removes hidden but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. Note: close() does set it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria-hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and close functions toggle the hidden attribute but do not toggle aria-hidden on the panel. The panel remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count update not announced to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge function updates the text content and aria-label of the badge but does not announce the change to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the product count changes.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and toggle state but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the context change.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list show function does not remove aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.show function removes the hidden attribute but does not remove aria-hidden from the listbox. The list remains hidden from assistive technology even when visible.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list hide function does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.hide function sets the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden on the listbox. The list remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top action not announced to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action to a live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden change in position.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher lacks keyboard handler for toggle elements
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init function adds a click listener to document for [data-theme-toggle] elements but does not add a keyboard handler (Enter/Space). If these elements are not native buttons, they are inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Skip link target #site-search not focusable

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has invalid aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has aria-modal="true", but this attribute is only valid on elements with role="dialog" or similar widget roles. Placing it on the overlay div is invalid ARIA and may confuse assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dialog element has role="dialog" but is missing aria-modal="true". This attribute is required for modal dialogs to inform assistive technology that content outside the dialog is inert.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has incorrect aria-modal and missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-modal="true"` attribute is placed on the backdrop overlay `div` instead of the element with `role="dialog"`, which prevents assistive technology from correctly scoping the modal content. Additionally, the overlay lacks `aria-hidden="true"`, potentially exposing the backdrop to the accessibility tree despite it being non-interactive for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1756: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `role="dialog"` is missing the `aria-modal="true"` attribute. This attribute is required to inform assistive technology that content outside the dialog is inert and should not be interacted with while the dialog is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1759: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: Modal focus restoration fails due to unused ref
issuesFound.explanation: The `demoTriggerRef` is created to restore focus when the modal closes, but it is passed to `SiteHeader` which does not accept or attach it to any DOM element. As a result, `closeDemoModal` attempts to focus `null`, leaving keyboard focus lost (often returning to `body`) when the dialog closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1912: const demoTriggerRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null); (Ensure this ref is attached to the button that opens the modal, e.g., in SiteHeader or LuminaryPage, rather than passed unused to SiteHeader)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: button component
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes `transform` in its transition property and applies a `translateY(1px)` transform on the `:active` state. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this spatial movement, which can cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447 (add after existing rules): `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: product card hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card:hover` selector applies a `transform: translateY(-3px)` spatial movement. The stylesheet lacks a `prefers-reduced-motion` media query to suppress this animation, potentially causing accessibility barriers for users with motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator such as box-shadow or border for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow, leaving high-contrast mode users without focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline and only sets outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for button elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for the mobile navigation toggle button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement, leaving dropdown menu links without visible focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes outline without providing a visible alternative, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing which account link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without providing a box-shadow or border replacement, overriding the :focus style and leaving no visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see a focus indicator on any element that relies on this global rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with no visible replacement. Users navigating in high-contrast mode will lose focus indicators entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible removes the default outline with outline: none and provides no visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menus cannot see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. The border remains transparent in focus state, leaving no visible indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 487: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with no replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 512: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px. An offset alone cannot create a visible focus ring when the outline width is zero.
issuesFound.fix: Line 326: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the global rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator. The outline-color property has no effect when outline-style is none.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus indicator for high-contrast mode users. Forced colors mode should preserve or enhance focus visibility, not remove it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset provided, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 746: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline in focus-visible state and provides only outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 894: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline in focus-visible state with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line 827: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size and falls in the 24–43px range that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 554: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA), making it nearly impossible for many users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 662: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: The large button variant sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to hit accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 669: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8, creating an inaccessible touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 676: min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a and button
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu items set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size and may cause accidental activations or misses for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for interactive elements and may be difficult for users to tap accurately on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 24px minimum and far below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes set width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8, making them difficult for users with motor impairments to select accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which keeps the content in the document flow and visible, failing to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the utility's purpose and may expose screen-reader-only text visually or hide it incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340-350: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transition: transform var(--transition-base) (line 393), which animates spatial movement. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience disorientation or nausea from this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 379: (add after existing transition) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition (line 1085) and has a hover transform (line 1090) that causes 3D spatial movement. No reduced-motion override exists to disable this for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1078: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards include transform in transitions (line 1217) and translate on hover (line 1224). These spatial animations lack a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card link arrow animates transform on hover (line 1286) with a transition (line 1278). This spatial movement lacks a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1276: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; } .feature-card a:hover::after { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items include transform in transitions (line 1743) and translate on hover (line 1750). These spatial animations may trigger vestibular issues without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards include transform in transitions (line 1909) and translate on hover (lines 1912, 1941). These spatial animations lack a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1902: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards include transform in transitions (line 1592) and translate on hover (line 1599). No reduced-motion override is provided for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion arrow animates transform (line 2317) with a transition (line 2312). This rotation lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2307: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can significantly reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by causing words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 359: word-spacing: 0; (or remove the property)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover, border-bottom, or font-weight change) for body-text links. This causes links to be distinguishable only by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons use inappropriate aria-selected attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) are native button elements with role="button" (implicit). The code sets both aria-pressed and aria-selected attributes on these buttons. aria-selected is not appropriate for button elements; it is intended for selectable items in lists, grids, or tabs. This creates conflicting state information for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3754-3756: Remove the line `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and rely solely on aria-pressed to indicate the active state.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides lack role="tabpanel" for tab pattern
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel implements a tab pattern using role="tab" on the dot navigation buttons with aria-controls pointing to slide IDs. However, the slide elements (article[data-slide]) are not marked with role="tabpanel". When a tab pattern is used, the content panels must have role="tabpanel" to properly convey the relationship to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4220 (within testimonialsCarousel.init): After collecting slides, add `slide.el.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` to each slide element.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not explicitly removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes the open CSS class from the drawer and sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, but it does not explicitly set aria-hidden="true" or the hidden attribute on the drawer element itself. If the CSS only translates the drawer off-screen rather than using display:none, the drawer content remains perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` within the close() function, and correspondingly add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` in the open() function around line 2820.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications lack user-controllable pause for auto-dismiss timer
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a configurable duration using setTimeout. While users can dismiss toasts early via the dismiss button, there is no mechanism to pause the auto-dismiss timer (e.g., on hover or focus), which may prevent users with reading disabilities from fully perceiving the notification content before it disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4515: Implement timer pause on mouseenter/focusin and resume on mouseleave/focusout events on the toast element, or provide an explicit pause button.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone role may not convey composite functionality
issuesFound.explanation: The drop zone is assigned role="button", which suggests a simple clickable button. However, the zone supports both click-to-browse and drag-and-drop file operations, and displays a list of selected files. The button role may not adequately convey the drop target functionality or the presence of the file list to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5120: Change role to "region" or "group" with aria-label describing it as a file upload area, and ensure the internal button (or the zone itself if clickable) maintains a clear accessible name. Alternatively, use role="application" with appropriate aria-roledescription, though ensuring the native file input remains accessible is preferable.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input status not linked via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The status message element (_statusEl) is created and updated with text content, but it is not programmatically associated with the discount code input field via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage. When validation feedback appears, screen reader users may not perceive the relationship between the input and the status message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4878: After creating _statusEl, add `srUtils.addDescribedBy(_inputEl, _statusEl);` to link the input to its status message.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels not marked as aria-hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: While the code sets aria-hidden on the leaving panel, it relies on the hidden attribute for the entering panel. However, if the wizard uses CSS transitions that require panels to remain in the DOM (not display:none), inactive panels may remain visible to screen readers unless explicitly marked with aria-hidden="true". The code sets aria-hidden on the leaving panel but should ensure all inactive panels have aria-hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line 5320: Add a loop to set `panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')` on all step panels except the current one during initialization and step changes.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button may lack accessible name in some configurations
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (_clearBtn) is queried from the DOM using SELECTORS.searchClear. If the HTML element lacks an aria-label or visible text, and the JavaScript does not ensure an accessible name is present, the button may be announced as "unlabeled" or by its symbol only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4055: Add a check and set `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) _clearBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger may not be recognized as button if not button element
issuesFound.explanation: The code attempts to use `qs('button, summary', el)` as a fallback for the trigger. If the trigger is a div or other element with role="button", the code adds tabindex="0" and role="button", but it does not ensure that the element is focusable via keyboard or that it handles Enter/Space keys. While the click handler is attached via delegation, keyboard activation requires explicit keydown handling for non-button elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4655: Ensure that if the trigger is not a native button or summary, explicit keyboard handlers for Enter and Space keys are added to trigger the toggle, or force the element to be a native button element.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggle lacks aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) are updated with aria-pressed when clicked, but the initial state of aria-pressed may not be set correctly on page load if the view mode is restored from storage. The _syncUrl or init function should initialize the aria-pressed state to match the current view mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4385: In the init function of productFilter, after restoring the saved view mode, add code to set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode));` for all view mode buttons.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification auto-dismiss lacks pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toast manager automatically dismisses notifications after a timeout using setTimeout without providing a mechanism for users to pause, stop, or hide the content. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) and 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide), as users with cognitive disabilities or those using screen readers may need more time to read the notification.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2404: Replace the setTimeout block with a pausable timer that clears on hover/focus and restarts on leave/blur:
  Line 2404: `let timerId = null; let isPaused = false; if (dur > 0) { timerId = setTimeout(() => { if (!isPaused) dismiss(toast); }, dur); toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { isPaused = true; }); toast.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { isPaused = false; if (timerId) clearTimeout(timerId); timerId = setTimeout(() => { if (!isPaused) dismiss(toast); }, dur); }); toast.addEventListener('focusin', () => { isPaused = true; }); toast.addEventListener('focusout', () => { isPaused = false; if (timerId) clearTimeout(timerId); timerId = setTimeout(() => { if (!isPaused) dismiss(toast); }, dur); }); }`

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger lacks keyboard activation for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: When the FAQ trigger is not a native `<button>` or `<summary>` element, the code adds `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` but fails to add keydown event listeners for Enter and Space keys. This prevents keyboard-only users from activating the accordion panels when the HTML uses non-button elements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3760: After setting tabindex, add a keydown listener to simulate click:
  Line 3760: `trigger.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); trigger.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison toggle missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `setComparisonTableExpanded` function sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger button but does not ensure `aria-controls` is set to reference the comparison table element. This breaks the programmatic relationship required by WCAG 4.1.2 for assistive technologies to understand which content is controlled by the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4298: Before calling setExpanded, ensure aria-controls references the table:
  Line 4298: `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) { const tableId = table.id || srUtils.ensureId(table, 'comparison-table'); setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', tableId); }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer implements a focus trap and obscures the main content, functioning as a modal dialog, but the code does not set `aria-modal="true"` on the drawer element. This prevents screen readers from conveying the modal context to users, which is required for proper navigation in modal contexts.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3092: After removing the hidden attribute from the drawer, add:
  Line 3092: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');`

fpTitles: Toast notification auto-dismiss lacks pause mechanism
FAQ trigger lacks keyboard activation for non-button elements
Pricing comparison toggle missing aria-controls
Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal

issuesFound.title: Inline validator error elements have conflicting ARIA live region attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The error message span is created with both `role: 'alert'` and `'aria-live': 'polite'`. The alert role implies an assertive live region, while the polite value indicates the opposite. This creates conflicting instructions for assistive technologies on how to announce validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2256-2262: Remove the `'aria-live': 'polite'` property, keeping only `role: 'alert'` (which implicitly provides `aria-live="assertive"`).

issuesFound.title: Honeypot input field lacks disabled state
issuesFound.explanation: The dynamically created honeypot input has `tabindex: '-1'` on its wrapper but the input itself is not disabled and lacks `aria-hidden="true"`. While the wrapper hides it from the accessibility tree, the input remains technically focusable via script and could be discovered by some assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2314-2320: Add `disabled: true` and `'aria-hidden': 'true'` to the input element attributes: `const input = createElement('input', { type: 'text', name, value: '', tabindex: '-1', autocomplete: 'off', disabled: true, 'aria-hidden': 'true' });`

issuesFound.title: File upload zone uses deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets `aria-dropeffect` to 'copy' and 'none' on the upload zone element. This attribute was deprecated in ARIA 1.1 and removed from later specifications. It is not supported by modern assistive technologies and provides no accessibility benefit.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2384: Remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'copy');` and Line 2390: Remove `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'none');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels have role tabpanel without corresponding tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step form sets `role="tabpanel"` on each step panel, but the navigation uses simple Previous/Next buttons rather than a tablist with `role="tab"` elements. This creates a mismatch where assistive technologies expect tab controls but find none, confusing the user about the navigation model.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2477: Remove `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` and the associated `aria-label` on line 2478. Use standard `hidden` attribute toggling without tab roles, or implement a full tab pattern with `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, and `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table headers use aria-selected outside tab context
issuesFound.explanation: The function sets `aria-selected="true"` on table header (`th`) elements. The `aria-selected` attribute is only valid on elements with specific roles such as `option`, `tab`, or `gridcell`. Table headers do not support this attribute, causing invalid ARIA usage.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2543: Remove `setAttr(th, 'aria-selected', 'true');` and the corresponding removal on line 2545. Use a data attribute or CSS class to manage the highlighted state visually instead.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar lacks aria-controls association
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element (`_progressBar`) has `role="progressbar"` and updates `aria-valuenow`, but it lacks `aria-controls` to indicate which step panel is currently active. This prevents assistive technologies from understanding the relationship between the progress indicator and the content being shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2433-2444: Add `setAttr(_progressBar, 'aria-controls', _steps[_current].panelEl.id);` after ensuring the panel has an ID, or set it once during initialization pointing to the steps container.

issuesFound.title: Search status region lacks semantic role
issuesFound.explanation: The dynamically created search status element has `aria-live="polite"` but no explicit `role` attribute. While functional, adding `role="status"` would provide better semantic clarity for assistive technologies and ensure consistent treatment across different browser/AT combinations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1968-1973: Add `role: 'status'` to the createElement call for `_statusEl`.

issuesFound.title: Character counter lacks semantic role
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter span uses `aria-live="polite"` to announce remaining characters, but lacks `role="status"`. This role would reinforce that the element provides status information about the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2284-2288: Add `role: 'status'` to the createElement call for `counterEl`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger conditionally lacks aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The code only sets `aria-controls` on the navigation toggle button if the drawer element already has an `id` attribute (`&& _drawer.id`). If the HTML markup lacks this ID, the critical relationship between the trigger and the drawer is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1124: Change the condition to ensure the drawer always has an ID: `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId); }`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons have redundant conflicting ARIA states
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are assigned both `aria-pressed` and `aria-selected` attributes simultaneously. `aria-pressed` indicates a toggle button state, while `aria-selected` indicates a selected item in a list (such as tabs). These are mutually exclusive patterns; having both creates confusion about the control type.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2621-2622: Remove `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and keep only the `aria-pressed` attribute, as these function as toggle buttons rather than list items.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function open(announceToSR) { ... _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing aria-expanded update consistency
issuesFound.explanation: While the close function sets aria-expanded="false", it is paired with an open function that fails to set the attribute to "true", creating an inconsistent accessibility state where the toggle appears collapsed to assistive technology even when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Ensure open() sets aria-expanded="true" as in Issue 1; close() already contains setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open routine removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing when the menu expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _openInstance(inst) { ... inst.open = true; setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function animates the panel height and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technology cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... item.open = true; setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, the function animates the panel closed and sets the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The button continues to report an expanded state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function close(item, announceToSR) { ... item.open = false; setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid when focus moves to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { ... addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function emits a custom event and updates CSS classes when the menu closes, but does not announce the state change via the live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the navigation has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function close(announceToSR) { ... emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); } }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu updates the DOM visibility but provides no auditory feedback via the live region. Users navigating with screen readers may not realize the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes it from the DOM visibility but does not announce the closure via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus(); liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is hidden via CSS class removal but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden is not set to "true". Screen readers can still access the closed menu content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function close(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); ... } and in open(): setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing Enter/Space keyboard handler for toggling
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion implements Arrow key navigation for moving between questions, but fails to handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the answer panel. Keyboard-only users cannot expand or collapse items without a mouse.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _onKeydown(e) { ... switch (e.key) { ... case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); const item = items[current]; if (item) toggle(item); break; ... } }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips.render() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are dynamically added or removed from the container, the function updates the DOM but provides no announcement via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current active filters.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu hover/click missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel missing focus management on slide change

issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes to display the drawer but never updates the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers cannot perceive that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not updated to "true", so screen readers report the item as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3159: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons lack aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid="true"

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() omits live-region announcement

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout without a way to pause, stop, or extend the timer, violating WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable).
issuesFound.fix: Add mouseenter/mouseleave and focusin/focusout listeners to the toast element that clear/reset the dismiss timer, or remove the auto-dismiss behavior entirely.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element has role="progressbar" and aria-valuenow, but lacks aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax attributes required by the ARIA specification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3622: Add `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` (or set max to the total number of steps).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden via CSS class removal but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden="true" is not applied when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2103: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close function. Line 2080: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`) in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded or collapsed state of the menu, leaving users unaware whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function: add "setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');" after addClass(_drawer, ...). Line close function: add "setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');" after removeClass(_drawer, ...).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows and hides panels by setting the hidden attribute, but it never toggles aria-expanded on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot convey the open or closed state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance function: add "setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');" after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'). Line _closeInstance function: add "setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');" at the start of the function before removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the inline validator adds error classes and inserts error text, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line _renderResult function (else branch): add "setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');" after srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes error styling but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line _renderResult function (if result.valid branch): add "setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');" after srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger not initialised with aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen readers will not know the initial collapsed state of the menu before it is first opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function (after _initialised = true): add "setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');".

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input not initialised with aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is created without an initial aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers cannot convey that the suggestion list is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function (after suggestionListRenderer.mount): add "setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not setting aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but aria-hidden="true" is not applied. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree, allowing screen readers to navigate to off-screen content.
issuesFound.fix: Line close function (after removeClass(_drawer, ...)): add "setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels not setting aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden using the hidden attribute, but the code does not also set aria-hidden="true". While the hidden attribute removes content from the accessibility tree in modern browsers, explicit aria-hidden ensures compatibility with older assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line _closeInstance function (after inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): add "setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');".

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestions not setting aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox is hidden using the hidden attribute without accompanying aria-hidden="true". Explicit aria-hidden is needed for consistent accessibility tree management across browser and assistive technology combinations.
issuesFound.fix: Line hide function (after _listEl.setAttribute('hidden', '')): add "setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');".

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panels not setting aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are hidden with the hidden attribute but lack aria-hidden="true". Explicit aria-hidden ensures the collapsed content is properly excluded from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line close function (after item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): add "setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');".

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing filter results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible card count updates and the grid changes, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the new filter or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line applyFilters function (inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge): add "liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);".

issuesFound.title: pricing not announcing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the displayed prices visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have updated or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod function (after _syncToggleState): add "liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);".

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion not announcing open/close via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items updates the visual state and aria-expanded, but no status message is announced. Users may not know whether the action succeeded, especially if focus management is unclear.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function (after emit): add "liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);". Line close function (after emit): add "liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu not announcing open/close via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus changes the visual state but provides no auditory feedback via live regions. Screen reader users may not perceive that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance function (after emit): add "liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');". Line _closeInstance function (after emit): add "liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap referenced but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable and checks if it is active in the close function, but the open function never creates or activates a focus trap. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page, breaking modal dialog focus containment.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function (after _lockScroll): add "_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not move focus to drawer on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users must manually navigate to the menu content, creating a poor experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function (after _trap.activate): add "const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus();".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line close function (after _unlockScroll): add "if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();".

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Users may lose their place in the page when the panel closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line close function (after removeClass): add "item.trigger.focus();".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element. Screen readers cannot establish the programmatic relationship between the button and the controlled navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function (after ensuring _drawer has an id): add "setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));".

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for personal information (name, email, etc.) are bound for validation but the JavaScript does not add autocomplete attributes. Browsers cannot offer autofill for these fields, reducing usability for users with cognitive disabilities or those relying on password managers.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function (after inlineValidator.bind for each field): add "if (field.name === 'email') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); if (field.name === 'full_name') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');" [and similar for other personal data fields].

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers from autofilling the user's email address, creating unnecessary typing effort.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function (after getting _emailEl): add "_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');".

issuesFound.title: Wizard form inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard collects personal data (name, email) but does not set autocomplete attributes on these fields. This hinders autofill and accessibility for users who rely on browser assistance.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function (inside the step.fields.forEach loop): add "if (cfg.selector.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); if (cfg.selector.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown menus to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2555: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside _openInstance().
  Line 2565: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;` inside _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function associates error messages with form fields via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Conversely, srUtils.markValid() does not remove the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2105: Insert `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` inside markInvalid().
  Line 2125: Insert `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` immediately after the aria-describedby cleanup logic inside markValid().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panels not removed from accessibility tree when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close() function hides the answer panel using the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". While the hidden attribute removes content from the accessibility tree in modern browsers, explicit aria-hidden ensures consistent behavior across all assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4285: Insert `item.panel.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` inside close().

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function sets the hidden attribute on panels but does not set aria-hidden="true". The _openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute but does not remove aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2575: Insert `inst.panel.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` inside _closeInstance().
  Line 2558: Insert `inst.panel.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` inside _openInstance().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.hide() function sets the hidden attribute on the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-hidden="true", leaving the collapsed list in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3450: Insert `_listEl.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` immediately after `_listEl.setAttribute('hidden', '');` inside hide().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer by removing the open CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element when it is closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2445: Insert `_drawer.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` inside close().
  Line 2425: Insert `_drawer.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` inside open().

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() function updates the visual active state of filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the active/inactive state. The HTML contract specifies these buttons should use aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3760: Insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside _syncTabAriaStates().

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function dynamically updates price values when users switch between monthly and annual billing, but no announcement is made via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3920: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed to ' + period);` immediately after updating the plan cards inside setPeriod().

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick() function scrolls the page to the top and moves focus, but does not announce the action via a live region. Users relying on screen readers may not understand that the page has scrolled to the top.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2660: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` immediately after the scroll command inside _onClick().

issuesFound.title: Filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips._onChipClick() function removes filter chips and updates the product grid, but does not announce the removal action or the resulting filter change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4170: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed: ' + label);` immediately after the filter is removed (before the switch statement break) inside _onChipClick().

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product cards and count badge when filters are applied, but does not announce the results via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many products are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3780: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside applyFilters().
