issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer trigger button does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated to "true" when the menu opens. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open and that they can navigate inside it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2417: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not reset to "false". Assistive technologies will continue to report the menu as expanded, confusing users about whether the navigation is currently available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2436: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function that opens a dropdown panel fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Users navigating with screen readers will not hear that the dropdown is open and that they can access its contents.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2557: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel closes, the trigger button retains its aria-expanded="true" state because the code never sets it to "false". This causes screen readers to incorrectly report the dropdown as still expanded after it has visually closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2572: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field fails validation, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding that their input contains errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3806: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the function removes the error description but leaves aria-invalid="true" (or fails to set it to "false"). Screen readers may continue to report the field as invalid even after the user has corrected their input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3825: Insert `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` (or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`) immediately before the closing brace of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false". Screen readers encounter the button without knowing its default collapsed state, violating the requirement that expandable controls communicate their state immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2489: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the code removes CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. If the CSS does not use display:none, the drawer remains perceptible to screen readers even when visually hidden, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2438: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` immediately after the line `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` and add corresponding `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` in the open() function after line 2419.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates and the result count changes visually, but no announcement is made via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4093: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` immediately after the line `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology will not know that the pricing information has changed or which billing period is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4397: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` immediately after the line `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has aria-modal instead of dialog container
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has aria-modal="true", but the dialog element (role="dialog") does not. Screen readers may not recognize the modal as modal because aria-modal must be on the same element as role="dialog".
